papers.title: Baden-Wurttemberg autopsy COVID-19 registry: a multisite FFPE and cryo-tissue biobank
papers.institution: Baden-Wurttemberg COVID Autopsy Registry / BBMRI-Eric Germany
papers.access_route: BBMRI-Eric sample locator; application to registry coordinating center; contact via GCS-Netz (German COVID autopsy network)
papers.notes: 4383 FFPE + 7766 cryopreserved tissue blocks from COVID-19 autopsies; cancer comorbidity documented (oncologic history at death); multi-organ sampling; linked to BBMRI-Eric infrastructure; de-identified with clinical metadata

papers.title: Cancer and COVID-19 co-occurrence: autopsy findings from Semmelweis University Budapest
papers.institution: Semmelweis University / 2nd Department of Pathology Budapest
papers.access_route: Collaborative agreement with Semmelweis University 2nd Pathology; IRB required; contact corresponding author
papers.notes: 100 cancer + COVID autopsy cases; multiple cancer types (lung, colorectal, hematologic); FFPE and frozen matched; COVID PCR status and severity documented; detailed oncologic history

papers.title: DeRegCOVID: a German autopsy registry for COVID-19 tissue research
papers.access_route: Application via DeRegCOVID portal (deregcovid.de); BBMRI-Eric linked; IRB and DUA required
papers.notes: 18,674 tissue samples from 1,129 autopsies across 12 German institutions; multi-organ; cancer comorbidity documented; COVID PCR confirmed; linked to clinical records; largest German COVID autopsy collection

expected_retrievable_institutions: Baden-Wurttemberg COVID Autopsy Registry / BBMRI-Eric Germany
notes: DeRegCOVID (n=18,674 tissues) and Baden-Wurttemberg registry (n=12,149) are the two largest collections with BBMRI-linked access. Both overlap with the same German network (GCS-Netz). Semmelweis provides a smaller Eastern European collection. All require formal application; cancer comorbidity is a documented field in all three.
